title: Call for Applications: 7th Batch of the IRDR Young Scientists Programme
content: The IRDR Young Scientists Programme is inviting early-career researchers to apply for its 7th batch, offering opportunities to join a global network and contribute to innovative disaster risk reduction research.

title: PROCULTHER-NET 2 Final virtual conference
content: The conference will bring together European and international experts to present the results of the PROCULTHER-NET project and discuss future strategies for strengthening cultural heritage protection within disaster risk management.

title: Participatory forensic investigations of disasters (PARFORIN): Including people in collective disaster risk analysis through social cartography
content: The Forensic Investigations of Disasters (FORIN) guide provides important approaches for research and extension purposes in order to understand and reduce disaster risks.

title: Typhoons: the hidden lifeline in a drying world
content: A research team led by Professor Jonghun Kam from POSTECH has revealed that typhoons are a critical factor in mitigating global droughts by simulating a scenario where typhoon-induced precipitation is removed.

title: When drought hits, do dams and groundwater soften the blow?
content: Droughts are the world’s most widespread natural disaster, and climate change is making them longer, more intense, and possibly more frequent. But how much do droughts actually hurt local economies and can water storage help cushion that blow?

title: “GeoDRR–2026: Geography of Disaster in the 21st Century: Challenges, Preparedness and Adaptations”
content: One-Day International Conference on GeoDRR–2026 Geography of Disaster in the 21st Century: Challenges, Preparedness and Adaptations (Hybrid Mode)

title: Resilience World Nexus (RWN) Summit 2026
content: The Resilience World Nexus Summit 2026 brings together global leaders to explore how resilience can drive performance, manage risk, and support long-term sustainable growth.

title: Resilience World Nexus
content: The Resilience World Nexus (RWN) Summit convenes C-suite executives and senior leaders who recognise that resilience drives performance, protects value, and secures long-term growth.

title: Half of people living in flood-prone areas never discuss climate risks with anyone, new international study finds
content: A study of 1,600 coastal residents in the Netherlands, UK, and US finds half never discuss flood or climate risks. Conversations are rare, driven mostly by friends and family, with women and those with flood experience more likely to engage.

title: 15 years after the Great East Japan earthquake & tsunami
content: Fifteen years after the 2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami, Japan’s recovery highlights the power of early warning, a strong culture of preparedness, and “build back better” reconstruction, shaping global disaster‑risk policy and regional cooperation.

title: How governments can help Indigenous communities disproportionately impacted by wildfires
content: Indigenous communities face disproportionate wildfire impacts due to limited resources, jurisdictional gaps, and evacuation challenges. The article urges long-term funding, better coordination, and stronger prevention and mitigation efforts.

title: Unplanned urbanisation fuels landslide disasters amid emerging rainfall trends
content: Researchers collaborated to assess the extent to which human-induced climate change altered the likelihood and intensity of the heavy rainfall event that caused the landslides.

title: Extreme heat has extreme effects – but some like it hot
content: A sweeping study of the 2021 heat wave reveals major ecological losses—but also surprising species that thrived, offering crucial insight into how climate extremes reshape ecosystems.

title: Reducing risk together: moving towards a more holistic approach to multi-hazard and multi-risk assessment and management
content: Moving towards a more holistic approach to disaster risk management, in which a multi-hazard and multi-risk approach is central, offers many opportunities to increase society's resilience.

title: A probabilistic performance-based framework for heat vulnerability and risk assessment of buildings
content: As climate-induced hazards increase rapidly, the built environment's limited preparedness highlights the urgent need to enhance resilience.

title: Better climate change adaptation through transformative innovation - enabling conditions and future perspectives
content: There is a growing scientific consensus that Climate Change Adaptation (CCA) is not being implemented at the scale, depth and speed needed to avoid dangerous climate-related risks.

title: Swiss mountainside fault grows as evacuation plans prepared
content: The “Fios fault” in the Val d’Anniviers region in southern Switzerland has now stretched to 250 metres and continues to grow. Authorities fear part of the mountainside could give way and are preparing safety measures and local evacuation plans.

title: Scaling up crop insurance in Africa for climate resilience and agricultural transformation
content: Crop insurance can help smallholder farmers in Africa and elsewhere manage climate risk and stabilize livelihoods in the face of droughts and extreme weather. Scaling coverage requires better product design, farmer education, and links to credit.

title: Program Manager - India
content: To support the effective implementation, coordination, and expansion of these initiatives, ADPC is seeking a Program Manager based in New Delhi, India.

title: As wildfires intensify, UB researchers test if AI can improve fire spread prediction
content: With wildfires growing more destructive both in the United States, University at Buffalo researchers have conducted one of the most extensive evaluations to date of artificial intelligence-based deep learning models for predicting wildfire spread.

title: Raising confidence in climate projections: The past to future approach
content: The Past to Future project seeks to validate climate models against a broader spectrum of past climate conditions. Learn why this approach is crucial for informing long-term infrastructure, land use, and financial planning.

title: Assessing vulnerability of rural buildings to tornadoes and their relationships with building attributes and surrounding land uses
content: This study presents an empirical assessment of rural building vulnerability to tornadoes in Yancheng City, Jiangsu Province, China, with a particular focus on how building attributes and surrounding land‑use patterns shape disaster risk.

title: Mental health crisis after 2023 Maui wildfires extends beyond burn zones
content: The 2023 Maui wildfires were linked to significantly higher levels of depression and anxiety among residents, with impacts extending beyond the burn zones and closely tied to housing and income disruption.

title: Half of people in Germany say their home is not ready for heatwaves – survey
content: Over half of Germans say their homes can’t handle rising heatwaves, a survey finds. Many also report poor indoor climates at work and want stricter building rules to improve health, comfort and climate resilience.

title: Massive 2011 Tohoku earthquake spurred development of modern tsunami forecast systems
content: PMEL's tsunami inundation forecast employs high-resolution models to deliver coastal tsunami forecasts, including predictions for amplitudes, inundation levels, and dangerous currents, covering all U.S. coastal population centers susceptible to tsunamis.

title: Beyond Temperature Brief
content: This report, “Beyond Temperature: A Cultural Context Approach to Heat and Health,” examines how heat-related illness is intensified by the intersection of environmental stress, biological vulnerability, and unequal power relations.

title: Research shows path to affordable water in fast-growing cities
content: A model of water supply, demand, and policies in a drought-prone city of 7 million in India shows how policies could prevent the poor from bearing the heaviest burden.

title: Victorian bushfires 2026: Longwood fire post-event field survey
content: A post‑event survey of the 2026 Longwood bushfire found extreme conditions led to rapid, widespread destruction, with 98% of affected buildings lost. Damage extended far beyond bushland edges due to ember attack and fast‑moving grassfires.

title: California communities’ recovery time between wildfire smoke events is shrinking
content: Californians have long dealt with wildfire smoke as a seasonal fact of life, but those fires have become more intense and frequent, raising the profile of wildfire smoke as a public health issue.

title: After a summer of weather disasters, will Kiwis make climate an election issue?
content: Despite severe 2026 weather disasters, climate change is unlikely to become a decisive election issue in New Zealand, as public concern typically fades quickly and voters prioritise cost of living and healthcare.

title: AI weather models show promise for hurricane forecasts, but Rice study finds key physical limitations
content: As AI tools play an expanding role in high-stakes hazard modeling, researchers at Rice University say an essential question remains: Do AI-generated storms behave realistically?

title: Measure resilience to unlock local-level DRR solutions
content: A resilience measurement framework, adaptable to any community and proven in its effectiveness, has the potential to address some of the most common challenges.

title: Broad analysis of river management accelerates natural solutions
content: In this paper, researcher Sien Kok (Deltares/WUR) shows that the way we assess plans strongly determines which measures are considered “best”. Her PhD research is part of the European MERLIN project, which promotes nature restoration in rivers.

title: In a world searching for solutions to the interconnected climate, biodiversity and land degradation crises, rangelands and pastoralists are part of the answer: FAO animal production and health expert
content: Interview with FAO's Director of Animal Production and Health on the role of rangelands and pastoralists in sustainable agrifood systems, biodiversity preservation and climate resilience.

title: From peril to system: fifteen years after Tohoku, are we prepared for the extreme?
content: Fifteen years after Tohoku, the article argues that despite better models and data, true resilience requires accepting structural uncertainty, stress‑testing interconnected risks, and ensuring reinsurance capital is ready for extreme, system‑wide shocks.

title: Managed retreat in Europe more widespread than previously thought
content: A new study published in Earth's Future shows that managed retreat, the planned, government-supported relocation of people and infrastructure away from flood-prone areas, is being practiced across Europe at larger scale than thought.

title: A predictive model to assess liability in earthquake disasters
content: This study presents a conceptual model that predicts when liability of stakeholders may be triggered by linking consequence severity and stakeholder preparedness with decisions, resource allocation, and governing laws.

title: Building a whole-of-government fire governance: the Portuguese Integrated Rural Fire Management System
content: This is a strategic plan that establishes an overarching framework for wildfire risk management in Portugal. The plan marks a structural change for rural prevention and suppression.

title: ‘Life is a Miracle,’ but learning from disasters isn’t: Lessons from Japan’s 2011 earthquake and tsunami
content: Japan’s 2011 disaster shows that physical defenses aren’t enough — lasting preparedness depends on memory, education, and community efforts that keep risk awareness alive long after the event.

title: Accelerating impact: a gender lens on adaptive social protection in the Sahel
content: Empowering women and girls is a strategic imperative for tackling the complex and interconnected challenges facing countries in the Sahel.
